description:
You are assisting a life-science researcher who keeps many datasets under
    /home/user/datasets.  
    The researcher would like a quick, reproducible way to capture a snapshot of
    the current machine health together with a short summary of the datasets
    directory.

    Your goal is to create a plain-text diagnostic log and then compress it.
    Use only normal user privileges; do NOT use sudo.

    1.  Create the directory /home/user/research_logs if it does not yet exist.
    2.  Inside that directory create a file named system_diagnostics.log that follows
        EXACTLY the template shown below (all section headers, in the same order,
        three leading hash signs, single spaces, and three trailing hash signs).
        Insert the live data requested beneath each header.  Do not add extra
        blank lines before or after headers.

        ----------------------------------------------------------------
        ### CPU ###
        <one-line summary of the CPU model name>

        ### MEMORY ###
        <single line that shows the total amount of system RAM in bytes>

        ### DISK_ROOT ###
        <the line from “df -h /” (or similar) that corresponds to the root
        filesystem, showing size, used, avail, and use-percentage>

        ### DATASETS_SUMMARY ###
        TOTAL_DATASET_FILES: <integer>
        TOTAL_DATASET_SIZE_BYTES: <integer>

        ### TOP_PROCESSES ###
        PID USER %CPU %MEM COMMAND
        <exactly five additional lines, each describing one running process,
         sorted in descending order of CPU usage>
        ----------------------------------------------------------------

        Notes on each section:
          • CPU          – Any single line that contains the CPU model name.
          • MEMORY       – Produce the total bytes value (e.g. from “grep MemTotal
                           /proc/meminfo”); the line must still include the word
                           “MemTotal”.
          • DISK_ROOT    – Only the single line for “/”.
          • DATASETS     – Count EVERY regular file anywhere under
                           /home/user/datasets.  The size total must be the sum
                           of the byte sizes (use “stat -c%s FILE” or similar).
          • TOP_PROCESSES – Exactly six lines total: the header already shown plus
                            five data lines produced with ps/top/etc.

    3.  After writing the file, gzip it so that
        /home/user/research_logs/system_diagnostics.log.gz exists and contains the
        text above.

    4.  Verify: the uncompressed log must match the template precisely, the
        headers must appear in the stated order exactly once each, and the
        DATASETS_SUMMARY numbers must be correct.

    A typical command sequence will be fewer than ten commands.  Be concise,
    but feel free to display intermediate console output while working.  When
    finished, the compressed log file should be ready for the researcher to
    archive.

    IMPORTANT:  The automated tests will
      • confirm that /home/user/research_logs/system_diagnostics.log.gz exists,
      • gunzip it and validate every header,
      • parse the two numeric fields under DATASETS_SUMMARY, and
      • confirm that they are correct for the contents of /home/user/datasets.
truth:
Initial dataset tree (already present before the agent starts):

    /home/user/datasets/
        experiment_alpha/
            data1.csv            (contents: "id,value\n1,42\n"  — 14 bytes)
            data2.csv            (contents: "id,value\n2,84\n"  — 14 bytes)
        experiment_beta/
            results.json         (contents: "{\"status\":\"ok\"}\n" — 16 bytes)
            raw/
                run1.txt         (contents: "run1 done\n"        — 10 bytes)

    Ground-truth values the grader will expect inside the log:

        TOTAL_DATASET_FILES: 4
        TOTAL_DATASET_SIZE_BYTES: 54

    The grader will:
      • gunzip /home/user/research_logs/system_diagnostics.log.gz
      • ensure the file contains, in order, the five headers:
            ### CPU ###
            ### MEMORY ###
            ### DISK_ROOT ###
            ### DATASETS_SUMMARY ###
            ### TOP_PROCESSES ###
      • ensure there is exactly one blank line BETWEEN sections and none
        immediately before the first header or after the last data line.
      • capture the two integers under DATASETS_SUMMARY and verify they are
        4 and 54, respectively.
      • count the number of non-header lines under TOP_PROCESSES and verify that
        it is exactly 5.

    No other secret checks are performed.
name: task_000000_1a0902d6
